nodes.label: P2.2 — the objective: class-aware (hard) negatives
nodes.description: Tests whether making SCFF's negative partner class-aware bends the depth-slope, holding P2.1's healthy transmission (layer-norm+linear+contrast) fixed. Grid: random (baseline) vs hard-oracle (true different class) vs hard-oracle-proto (different-class mean) vs hard-unsup (different KMeans cluster, k=10).

nodes.label: P2.2's negative-partner grid
nodes.description: random(base) slope -0.020; hard-oracle slope -0.022 (lift vs random +0.003, disjoint-IQR -0.010, not real); hard-proto -0.015 (+0.016, not real); hard-unsup -0.022 (+0.005, not real). KMeans cluster purity only 0.226 (chance 0.10) — clusters by pixel appearance not class. Synth control: oracle DOES lift there (+0.027, real).

nodes.label: P2.2 verdict: DEPTH IS NOT THE LEVER (decisive negative)
nodes.description: Even a perfect label-oracle negative fails to bend the depth-slope (-0.022, identical to random). The class signal is read at L1 but dilutes with depth rather than compounding, because each layer optimizes its own myopic goodness with no cross-layer coordination signal — a structural gap forward-only locality cannot supply.

nodes.label: Decision: close deep-SCFF static path -> route to P2.5 (multi-block)
nodes.description: Two decisive rungs (transmission P2.1 + objective P2.2, oracle-tight) both fail -> depth is intrinsic to SCFF's forward-only locality (later refined in Phase 3 to 'intrinsic to the energy objective'). P2.3 (collaboration) and P2.4 (interface) are now moot. Route to P2.5: get depth from GD-corrected boosted blocks, not a deep SCFF stack.
nodes.rationale: Confirms the architecture's own design logic: SCFF is the cheap shallow 80%, GD is the coordinating 20% that supplies cross-layer coordination.

hyperedges.description: P2.1 (transmission, norm x goodness grid) and P2.2 (objective, even a perfect label oracle) are the two decisive rungs that, together and only together, prove the depth wall is not fixable by any lever inside the energy-goodness family -- routing the architecture to boosted shallow blocks instead of a deep SCFF stack.
